title: No VPI access to interface members when using --public-depth instead of --public-flat-rw

features.name: systemverilog interface with signal members
features.description: Code should include a SystemVerilog interface declaration with signal members (e.g., tdata, tvalid, tready) inside the interface body

features.name: vpi access to interface member signals
features.description: Code should include a DUT that instantiates a SystemVerilog interface, where VPI code attempts to access interface member signals (e.g., dut.s_axis_if.tdata)

features.name: public_depth flag for signal visibility
features.description: Code should include a signal accessed via VPI under --public-depth compilation, where the signal is at depth N in the DUT hierarchy (depth 1 for top-level interface signals)
